name: Location & Maps
promptInjection:
CAPABILITY: Location & Maps (CoreLocation + MapKit)

PERMISSION:
- Create CLLocationManager in a ViewModel or dedicated LocationManager class. Set delegate.
- Call requestWhenInUseAuthorization() — handle .authorizedWhenInUse, .denied, .notDetermined in the delegate.
- Set desiredAccuracy = kCLLocationAccuracyBest (or kCLLocationAccuracyHundredMeters for battery savings).
- Handle .denied with a "Go to Settings" button.
- Do NOT request .authorizedAlways unless the app genuinely needs background location.

SWIFTUI MAP (iOS 17+):
- Use the new Map(position:) { } syntax with MapContentBuilder.
- @State var cameraPosition: MapCameraPosition = .automatic
- Map(position: $cameraPosition) { Marker("Title", coordinate: coord) }
- User location dot: Map { UserAnnotation() } — requires location permission.
- Custom annotations: Annotation("Title", coordinate: coord) { Image(systemName: "star.fill") }
- Programmatic camera: cameraPosition = .region(MKCoordinateRegion(center: coord, span: MKCoordinateSpan(latitudeDelta: 0.05, longitudeDelta: 0.05)))

DO NOT use these deprecated patterns:
- Map(coordinateRegion: $region) — deprecated on iOS 17+, use Map(position:) instead.
- MKAnnotationView subclassing — use SwiftUI Annotation() instead.
- MKMapView with UIViewRepresentable — only if you need features not in SwiftUI Map (e.g., custom overlays).

CLLocationManagerDelegate:
- Implement as a class conforming to NSObject, CLLocationManagerDelegate.
- locationManager(_:didUpdateLocations:) — extract locations.last?.coordinate.
- locationManagerDidChangeAuthorization(_:) — check manager.authorizationStatus.
- Stop updates when no longer needed: manager.stopUpdatingLocation().

WORKOUT / ROUTE RECORDING AND DISPLAY:
- To record a route: store an array of (CLLocationCoordinate2D, timestamp) or (coordinate, heartRate, elapsedTime) as the user moves; sample every few seconds via locationManager(_:didUpdateLocations:).
- To draw the route on the map: use MapPolyline(coordinates:) in SwiftUI Map (iOS 17+); for a polyline colored by value (e.g. heart rate zones: green=low, yellow=moderate, red=high), split the route into segments by value and draw multiple MapPolyline segments with different stroke colors, or use MKMapView with MKPolyline and a custom MKOverlayRenderer that colors by segment.
- Tapping a point on the route to show details: store waypoints with (coordinate, heartRate, pace, elapsedTime); use Annotation for each tapable waypoint or a single overlay with hit-testing; on tap show a sheet or popover with the stored stats for that point.
- Summary card: compute total distance (sum of segment distances from coordinates), average/max heart rate, duration, and estimated calories from the recorded data.

ANTI-PATTERNS:
- Do NOT create CLLocationManager inside a View body.
- Do NOT forget to call stopUpdatingLocation() — drains battery.
- Do NOT use the deprecated Map(coordinateRegion:) API.

COMPILER / SWIFT: Any ViewModel used with @StateObject must conform to ObservableObject. Any file using UIViewRepresentable or UIView must import UIKit; makeUIView(context: Context) requires SwiftUI for Context.
antiPatterns: Using deprecated Map(coordinateRegion:) on iOS 17+ (use Map(position:) instead)
Creating CLLocationManager inside a SwiftUI View body
Not handling the .denied authorization case with a Settings link

commonErrors: generic struct 'StateObject' requires that 'MeasureViewModel' conform to 'ObservableObject'
